nodes.context: Criminal organization based in Veneto, Italy, involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and extortion.
Predecessor criminal organization active in Veneto during the 1970s and 1980s, founded by Felice Maniero.
Affiliated groups of nomad criminals from Vicenza, Padova, and Venice involved in bank heists, robberies, drug trade, and kidnapping.
Powerful group based in Mestre with strong ties to Mala del Brenta, involved in drug trafficking and other criminal enterprises.
Old criminal underworld of Venice headed by the Rizzi brothers and Giancarlo Millo, engaged in similar activities as Mestrini.
Cartel under Silvano Maritan, protégé of Cosa Nostra boss Salvatore Contorno, involved in violent confrontations with rival cartel.
Sicilian mafia organization; allies of Nuova Mala del Brenta and source of cocaine.
Neapolitan mafia organization; associates operating in Veneto region.
Calabrian mafia organization; believed to have associates in Veneto region.
Sicilian mafia organization; colluded with Brenta mafiosi in fraudulent scams at a Maltese casino in 2002.
Defunct Neapolitan mafia organization; listed as ally of Nuova Mala del Brenta.

edges.context: Nuova Mala del Brenta emerged as a successor to the original Mala del Brenta.
Mestrini had strong ties to the Mala del Brenta.
Clan Giostrai were incorporated under the central authority of the Mala del Brenta.
Veneziani were incorporated under the central authority of the Mala del Brenta.
San Donà di Piave cartel was under Silvano Maritan, protégé of Cosa Nostra boss, and later gave percentage of assets to Maniero.
Nuova Mala del Brenta bought cocaine directly from Sicilian Mafia and had associates from Cosa Nostra.
Nuova Mala del Brenta had associates from Camorra operating in Veneto.
Nuova Mala del Brenta is believed to have associates from 'Ndrangheta.
Brenta mafiosi colluded with Stidda members in fraudulent scams at a Maltese casino in 2002.
Nuova Camorra Organizzata is listed as an ally of Nuova Mala del Brenta.
